diary:
Monday was a solid day, even if it felt a bit fragmented. The drive to Tukwila was smooth in the morning—I caught the tail end of people talking about the USA-Wales match at the shop. Honestly, I wasn't thinking about soccer, just focused on getting through a pile of brake adjustments and a derailleur that wouldn't cooperate.

Lunch at Molly Moon's was a nice break. I grabbed a sandwich and sat outside for a bit, watching Capitol Hill wake up. The weather was perfect, which made the afternoon commute back to Tukwila feel less annoying than it could've been. The light rail closure didn't really impact me since I'm driving anyway.

By the time I got home, I was tired but restless. Spent some time at my pottery wheel—just throwing clay, not really making anything intentional. There's something meditative about it after being on my feet all day. Ended the night feeling grounded, if that makes sense.
thought_process: The World Cup match didn't pull me in the way it seems to have grabbed others—I'm not really a soccer person, and the shop was busy enough that I didn't catch much buzz about it. The light rail closure was irrelevant to my day since I drive, so that freed up mental space. What shaped my mood was the rhythm itself: two commutes felt long, but breaking them up with lunch and errands in Capitol Hill actually helped rather than hurt. By evening, I needed something tactile and quiet. Pottery has become my reset button after long shifts. I chose to lean into that rather than trying to force energy for socializing or watching the match downtown. The day felt productive but also sustainable—I'm learning when to push and when to let myself just be.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
